intro: The CreateSheetFromObject method provides a high-level abstraction for writing collections of objects directly to Excel sheets. This approach automatically maps object properties to columns and applies attributes-based configuration without manual row-by-row iteration.
benefits: This feature is especially useful when you need to export structured data from your application with minimal boilerplate code. By using class attributes to decorate properties, you gain fine-grained control over column appearance, data type handling, styling, and even conditional formatting rules—all declaratively.
attributesIntro: Properties can be decorated with the following attributes to customize their Excel representation:

attributes.Ignore: ExcelIgnore completely excludes a property from being written to the sheet.
attributes.ColumnName: ExcelColumnName sets a custom column header name instead of using the property name.
attributes.ColumnOrder: ExcelColumnOrder controls the column order by specifying a zero-based position.
attributes.ColumnType: ExcelColumnType specifies the cell data type: Text, Number, or Formula.
attributes.ColumnWidth: ExcelColumnWidth sets a custom column width in characters.
attributes.ColumnHidden: ExcelColumnHidden hides the column from view in the generated spreadsheet.
attributes.StyleFormat: ExcelStyleFormat applies a named style to individual data cells in the column.
attributes.HeaderStyleFormat: ExcelHeaderStyleFormat (class-level) applies a named style to the entire header row.
attributes.ConditionalFormatFormula: ExcelConditionalFormatFormula applies conditional formatting based on a formula expression.
attributes.ConditionalFormatCellIs: ExcelConditionalFormatCellIs applies conditional formatting based on cell value comparisons (equal, between, greater than, etc.).
attributes.ConditionalFormatDuplicatedValues: ExcelConditionalFormatDuplicatedValues highlights cells that contain duplicate values within the column.

advancedIntro: The CreateSheetFromObject method supports several optional parameters for greater flexibility:

options.WriteHeaderRow: writeHeaderRow (default: true) controls whether column headers are written as the first row.
options.AddAutoFilter: addAutoFilterOnFirstColumn (default: false) automatically adds an autofilter to the header row for interactive filtering.
options.SheetVisibility: sheetState allows you to control sheet visibility: Visible, Hidden, or VeryHidden.
options.SharedStrings: useSharedStringsOnTextData (default: false) reduces file size by deduplicating text values across sheets.
